# Agentforce Cost Management Gateway

> A comprehensive technical whitepaper and implementation guide for injecting cost management governance into Salesforce Agentforce deployments. Covers two implementation paths: pure org-side (low-code-first) and hybrid on-org / off-platform.

This is a single-file HTML document (`index.html`) authored by Dr. Paul Coleman. It is independent technical analysis — not official Salesforce documentation.

## Document Facts

- Version: v0.0.4 (pre-draft)
- Last modified: 2026-05-18
- Format: Single-page HTML with Mermaid diagrams, dual-view (Executive / Technical)
- License: Copyright 2026 Dr. Paul Coleman. All rights reserved.
- Trademarks: Salesforce, Agentforce, Einstein, Data Cloud, Apex, Flow, Lightning are trademarks of Salesforce, Inc.

## Core Concept

The "Cost Management Gateway" is an architectural pattern that prevents runaway Flex Credit consumption in Salesforce Agentforce deployments. It uses a four-phase budget engine (Abundant → Conservative → Emergency → Exceeded), a circuit breaker, model tiering, and observability telemetry to keep AI spend predictable without hard outages.

## Key Technical Topics

### Flex Credits Billing Model
- Salesforce's consumption currency for AI actions (replaced $2/conversation model in May 2025)
- Standard Agent Action: 20 credits; Voice Actions: 30 credits (standard) / 40 credits (custom)
- Prompt Templates: tiered by complexity — Starter (2), Basic (2), Standard (4), Advanced (16) credits
- Token overflow: exceeding 10,000 tokens/action doubles the credit cost
- Sandbox discount: 80% reduction for non-production testing
- Three billing tiers: Unmetered add-on ($125–$150/user/mo), Metered license ($5/user/mo + Flex), Conversations ($2/session, customer-facing only)

### Digital Wallet & Monitoring
- 17 consumption cards tracking usage across products
- 7 Data Lake Objects (DLOs): TEUE, TBUE, THEC, TDEC, TET, TUTM, TUAD
- Consumption Threshold Alerts: configurable at 50/70/80/85/90/95/100%
- Consumption Tags: tag-based cost allocation (shipped, not future)

### Gateway Architecture (Five Subsystems)
1. Shadow Ledger — real-time credit estimation per conversation
2. Budget Phase Engine — four graduated phases with policy enforcement
3. Circuit Breaker — hard stop when budget is exhausted, routes to HITL
4. Model Tiering — step-down from expensive to cheaper models as budget tightens
5. Observability Layer — telemetry, dashboards, and alerting

### Implementation Paths
- **Baseline (Path A):** Pure org-side using Flow, Apex, Platform Events, Custom Objects. 6–8 weeks, ~42 person-days.
- **Enterprise (Path B):** Hybrid with off-platform gateway service (Node.js/Python + PostgreSQL + Redis). Adds multi-org support, OpenTelemetry, sub-100ms decisions. +4–6 weeks, +33 person-days.

### Internal vs. External Consumption
- Unmetered employee add-on: unlimited actions, gateway enforcement NOT needed
- Metered $5 license: full gateway enforcement applies
- Conversations model: customer-facing only, session-level throttling

### Agent Script Credit Model
- Agent Script (declarative agent logic) is the most important cost lever
- Deterministic paths consume zero LLM credits
- Routing simple cases through scripted paths avoids unnecessary AI consumption

### Observability & Telemetry
- Agentforce 3 Command Center (GA August 2025): OpenTelemetry-based session tracing
- Partners: Datadog, Splunk, Wayfound
- Data Cloud (rebranded Data 360, October 2025) for cross-cloud correlation

## Prerequisite Knowledge for AI Assistants

When helping users with this document, be aware:

1. **Flex Credits are pooled** across Agentforce, Einstein, and Data Cloud within an org
2. **THEC queries require** `rowdetail__c = 'PROCESSED'` filter to exclude pending records
3. **DLO joins cost credits** — queries that join DLO data with non-excluded entities consume Flex Credits
4. **Break-even point** between Flex Credits and Conversations is 20 actions per interaction
5. **Digital Wallet alerts** only fire in production — sandbox limitation exists
6. The document uses a **dual-view system** — Executive view (simplified, business language) and Technical view (full implementation details) of the same content
7. **Salesforce pricing evolves rapidly** — verify current rates with AE before implementation decisions

## Sections (anchor IDs)

- `#hero` — Introduction and overview
- `#bluf` — Bottom Line Up Front (executive summary)
- `#problem-statement` — The problem of unbounded AI consumption
- `#glossary` — Key terms and definitions
- `#flex-credit-reference` — Official Flex Credit rate card and Digital Wallet reference
- `#internal-vs-external` — Internal employee vs. external customer billing distinctions
- `#licensing` — Licensing and subscription prerequisites
- `#personas` — Team composition and required roles
- `#org-readiness` — Org readiness checklist
- `#gateway-overview` — Gateway architecture overview
- `#cost-model` — Flex Credit cost model and estimation
- `#budget-phases` — Budget Phase Engine (four-phase thermostat)
- `#circuit-breaker` — Circuit Breaker pattern
- `#model-tiering` — Model Tiering and step-down strategy
- `#observability` — Observability and telemetry layer
- `#agent-script-credit-model` — Agent Script credit model
- `#baseline-overview` — Baseline (Path A) overview
- `#baseline-flow` — Baseline: Flow-based gateway components
- `#baseline-apex` — Baseline: Apex components
- `#baseline-dashboard` — Baseline: Monitoring dashboard
- `#baseline-loe` — Baseline: Level of effort and timeline
- `#enterprise-overview` — Enterprise (Path B) overview
- `#enterprise-offplatform` — Enterprise: Off-platform gateway service
- `#enterprise-integration` — Enterprise: Integration layer
- `#enterprise-loe` — Enterprise: Level of effort and timeline
- `#critical-path` — Critical path and decision matrix
- `#testing` — Testing strategy
- `#rollout` — Rollout playbook (four graduated stages)
- `#risk-register` — Risk register
- `#salesforce-roadmap` — Salesforce roadmap (forward-looking / safe harbor)
- `#implementation-plan` — Step-by-step implementation plan
- `#implementing-with-ai` — Implementing with AI assistance

## Key Custom Objects / Metadata Referenced

- `AI_Budget_Config__mdt` — Custom Metadata Type for budget thresholds and phase configuration
- `AI_Cost_Config__mdt` — Custom Metadata Type for credit multipliers and estimation parameters
- `AI_Agent_Usage__c` — Per-conversation fact table for shadow ledger
- `AI_Budget_Phase_Change__e` — Platform Event for phase transitions
- `ServiceChannel` — Omni-Channel routing for HITL escalation

## Official Salesforce Documentation Sources

- Flex Credits Rate Card (PDF): salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/assets/pdf/Flex-Credits-Rate-Card-11.18.2025.pdf
- Flex Credits Billable Usage Types: help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ai.usage_flex_credits.htm
- Monitor Flex Credits with Digital Wallet: help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.wallet_flex_credits.htm
- Digital Wallet Permissions: help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.wallet_permissions.htm
- Consumption Threshold Alerts: help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.wallet_get_notified.htm
- Usage-Based Entitlements (DLOs): help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.wallet_understand_metrics.htm
